title: Evidence Label Auditor
purpose: Find obvious local/static evidence-label misuse in repo docs, routine specs, routine reports, and ledger-shaped JSON with deterministic named policy/eval rules, without mutating repository state.
trigger: Run after routine reports, docs, roadmap text, or routine specs change, especially before publishing proof claims or accepting delegated handoffs.
allowedActions: inspect repo-local docs and JSON read-only
parse routine specs for direct evidence reservations
scan text for explicit weak-evidence wording near strong proof wording
treat glossary, prohibition, and blocked-definition wording as allowed negatives
write a RoutineRunReport JSON when requested
forbiddenActions: stage, commit, merge, push, tag, or release
dispatch sessions or start nested subagents
walk hidden user config outside the inspected repository
claim semantic proof of wrongdoing from heuristic text matches
gates: routine specs parsed for explicit direct-evidence reservations
repo-local docs, specs, routine reports, and ledger-shaped JSON scanned with deterministic named rules ELA001-ELA009
RoutineRunReport JSON evidence buckets checked for direct, proxy, local, and blocked keys
local report includes rule-hit summary counts when findings are present

evidence.direct: Reserved for future rendered, runtime, or human-reviewed proof; the MVP does not emit direct proof.
evidence.proxy: Reserved for accepted external audit indexes or review artifacts; the MVP normally does not emit proxy proof.
evidence.local: Local static scan of repo docs, routine specs, routine reports, and ledger-shaped JSON, including deterministic rule-hit summaries.
evidence.blocked: Repository, routine specs, docs, or JSON audit inputs cannot be inspected safely.

escalation: Treat findings as local/static suspicions until a reviewer confirms the wording or report-shape issue.
Stop if the repository or routine specs cannot be inspected.
Do not edit docs or reports from this routine; report drift and let a separate implementation pass fix it.
